title: Mass. woman killed in 6-vehicle crash in Florida
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 69-year-old Massachusetts woman was killed in a six-vehicle crash on Interstate 75 in Ochopee, Florida.
The crash, involving a semi-truck, occurred just before noon, causing multiple serious injuries and one fatality.
The accident is under investigation by the Florida Highway Patrol, with no names of those involved released yet.

title: Former Miss Hall’s School teacher at center of grooming allegations is charged with rape
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matthew Rutledge, former Miss Hall’s School teacher, indicted on three counts of rape related to sexual abuse allegations.
Allegations surfaced nearly two years ago; Rutledge accused of grooming and assaulting students from 1991 to 2024.
Aleta Law's investigation linked Rutledge to five accounts of misconduct, leading to the indictment by Berkshire County DA.

title: FIFA reduces hotel room bookings in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: FIFA is reducing hotel room bookings in all World Cup host cities, including Boston, due to inflated initial estimates.
Boston is to host seven World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium from June 13 to July 9, 2026.
FIFA's reduction allows hotels to sell released rooms to transient visitors, potentially benefiting Boston's tourism.

title: ‘I had just lost everything’: Kelsey Fitzsimmons testifies in her trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover officer, testified to being shot while officers served a restraining order.
The bench trial is overseen by Judge Jeffrey Karp, with closing arguments scheduled for Thursday.
Fitzsimmons described contemplating suicide upon realizing the restraining order's implications for her personal and professional life.

title: In the midst of the MVP race, Jaylen Brown explains his stance on the NBA’s 65-game rule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown believes the NBA's 65-game rule for award eligibility might be slightly high.
Cade Cunningham, pivotal for Detroit Pistons' rise, may miss MVP consideration due to 61 games played.
Brown supports having a game requirement; at least 62 games played would be more reasonable to him.

title: ‘It felt like a gut punch’: Pride flags removed from BU offices ignite free speech, inclusion debate
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston University removed Pride flags from offices, citing a policy that bans outward-facing signage as content-neutral.
Faculty criticized BU's policy enforcement, claiming it selectively targets LGBTQ+ symbols but not other signs like posters.
BU's decision sparked wider debate on free speech, with discussions at a Boston City Council hearing on transparency and representation.

title: David Ortiz won Boston.com’s bracket of the best sports soundbites, and it wasn’t close
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: David Ortiz's 2013 'This is our f****** city' speech won Boston.com's bracket for the best sports soundbite.
Ortiz's soundbite won by the largest margin, beating Larry Lucchino's 'Evil Empire' quote in the second round.
Kevin Millar's 'Don't let us win today' made an unexpected run to the championship round from 11th seed.

title: A JetBlue flight reportedly hit a coyote during takeoff at T.F. Green Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: A JetBlue Airbus A320 hit a coyote during takeoff at T.F. Green Airport on March 24.
The flight to JFK returned for inspection; no issues or injuries were reported after police and EMS checked.
After a safe return and clearance, the flight resumed and landed at JFK International Airport without further incident.

title: Boston runner to take on 2026 Marathon for Corey C. Griffin Foundation
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Annie Yardley, a Boston resident, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Corey C. Griffin Foundation.
Yardley, 30, transitioned from non-athlete to marathon runner, inspired by the foundation's mission.
The Corey C. Griffin Foundation supports children's education and healthcare, helping them imagine new futures.

title: Cape Cod man charged with sister’s murder
author: John Waller
bullets: Kenneth Beltrame, 67, of Sandwich, accused of murdering his 61-year-old sister, Jennifer Beltrame.
Jennifer Beltrame was found dead Tuesday in her Sandwich home with signs of violent trauma.
Kenneth Beltrame to be arraigned in Barnstable District Court; investigation by police and DA’s office is ongoing.
